hero.tagline2: Complex business logic converted into infrastructure that runs on its own.
hero.subtitle: I design CRM/ERP integrations, migrate automation pipelines, and build internal AI-powered tooling. I work with teams that need their systems to scale without scaling the team.

bio.p1: Automation and integration engineer with full technical ownership of CRM/ERP ecosystems. I design architecture — CQRS, hexagonal, idempotency, reconciliation — and execute from integration design through deployment.
bio.p2: I work with explicit patterns and documented conventions. The systems I deliver are operable and extensible without my constant involvement.

case1.title: Multi-tenant read model over CRM — CQRS pattern
case1.problem: A CRM as the sole system of record doesn't scale for serving data to external portals, analytics agents, and clients without degrading the transactional core or exposing cross-tenant data.
case1.approach: Command/query separation: the CRM remains the system of record while a cloud data store serves as the dedicated read model. Event-driven ingestion with a single topology, API Gateway as the sole external boundary, tenant ID indexed at row level, watermark-based reconciliation for eventual consistency.
case1.impact: Portals and agents query without touching the CRM. Tenant isolation guaranteed at the data level. The transactional core handles zero external read load.
case1.stack: Zoho CRM · Zoho Catalyst (Data Store, Functions, API Gateway) · Node.js

case2.title: Automation migration from SaaS to self-hosted platform
case2.problem: Critical business flows — electronic invoicing, data normalization, scheduling — running on a low-code SaaS with variable per-task cost, growing lock-in, and no versioning capability.
case2.approach: Incremental migration to n8n, flow by flow, zero downtime. Includes redesigning a data normalization step with an LLM — abbreviation rules, accent normalization, structured fallbacks — and fixing bugs inherited from the previous platform's data wrapping.
case2.impact: Variable per-task cost eliminated. Full control over flow versioning and testing. Audit trail and rollback capability the previous platform didn't support.
case2.stack: n8n · OpenAI API · Zoho CRM · Zoho Books · AR fiscal APIs

case3.title: AI-assisted internal tooling for support and reporting
case3.problem: Operational knowledge — incidents, module behavior, CRM structure — lived in one person's head. First-level support escalated everything.
case3.approach: Custom conversational skills built on Claude: one for operational support with an incident knowledge base and a three-tier response system (documented match / partial match / declared inference), and one for reporting that generates structural and data analysis for any CRM module with corporate PDF output.
case3.impact: First-level support resolved without escalation. Reports that took hours now generated in minutes. Tacit knowledge converted into a queryable, auditable system.

items.title: URL shortener with integrated analytics
items.description: Self-hosted URL shortener with click tracking integrated into the CRM. Access records feed in real time into the sales team's data pipeline.

items.title: FIFO portfolio reconciliation
items.description: Automated portfolio reconciliation system using FIFO methodology. Processes transactions, detects mismatches, and generates per-client status reports.

items.title: Hexagonal patterns for CRM and automation
items.description: Development framework for structuring business logic in CRM and automation platforms under hexagonal architecture. Separates domain, adapters, and ports to make flows testable and reusable.

items.title: Internal and external communication integration
items.description: System that centralizes internal (Zoho Cliq) and external (Respondio) communication into the CRM, with routing rules and automatic interaction logging.

items.title: Advanced filtering management table
items.description: Nested table component with advanced filtering built with Quasar Framework, deployed in a portfolio management system for insurance producers.

bio.p1: Automation and integration engineer with full technical ownership of CRM/ERP ecosystems. I design architecture — CQRS, hexagonal, idempotency, reconciliation — and execute from integration design through deployment.
bio.p2: My deepest areas are critical business flow automation, platform responsibility separation — CRM as system of record, cloud data stores as the read layer — and internal tooling design that reduces dependence on tacit knowledge. I prefer systems the team can audit, version, and extend on their own.
